name: Reinosa - Fontibre - Argüeso por el Natural Way of the Ebro
name_original: Reinosa - Fontibre - Argüeso por el Camino Natural del Ebro
region_intro: Hiking around Reinosa offers access to diverse natural landscapes in the mountainous region of Cantabria, Spain. The area features a mix of high peaks, tranquil river origins, and dense woodlands, providing varied scenery for hikers. Reinosa is strategically located, offering routes that explore ancient forests, the source of the Ebro River, and provide views of surrounding mountain ranges. The terrain includes paths suitable for casual strolls and more demanding trails with significant elevation gain. Best hiking trails around Reinosa The most popular hiking route is Reinosa - Fontibre - Argüeso por el Natural Way of the Ebro, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows part of the Natural Way of the Ebro, showcasing diverse landscapes along the river valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plaza de España, Reinosa – Source of the Ebro River loop from Río Ebro, a moderate 7.4 miles (12.0 km) path. This trail explores the area around the traditionally recognized origin of the Ebro River. Local hikers also love the Historic Center of Reinosa – Plaza de España, Reinosa loop from Río Ebro, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through the historic center and along the Ebro River, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Reinosa is defined by high peaks, river origins, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Reinosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Reinosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Reinosa?
A: Reinosa offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy, accessible paths perfect for a casual stroll to more demanding routes requiring good fitness. You'll find paths through ancient forests, along river sources, and up to high peaks with panoramic views. The region has 21 trails, with 10 easy, 10 moderate, and 1 difficult route.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Reinosa?
A: Yes, Reinosa has several easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31885896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa – Plaza de España, Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.1 km long and takes around 1 hour 50 minutes. This route allows you to explore the town's historic center and walk along the Ebro River. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Bridge – Parish Church of San Sebastián loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, also around 7.1 km.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Reinosa?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Reinosa are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España, Reinosa – Source of the Ebro River loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.0 km circular path that explores the area around the Ebro's source. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31885896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa – Plaza de España, Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Reinosa?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. A must-see is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; in Fontibre, the traditionally recognized origin of one of Spain's most significant rivers. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Roman city, or experience a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;beautiful section through the beech forest&lt;/a&gt;, especially vibrant in autumn.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Reinosa offers several opportunities for panoramic views. The region research mentions Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares as areas with impressive vistas, including views of the Cantabrian interior, Picos de Europa National Park, and Montaña Palentina Natural Park. While not a specific komoot route listed, the more challenging trails in the area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Bridge – Source of the Ebro River loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, often feature significant elevation gain that can lead to rewarding views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Reinosa?
A: The hiking trails in Reinosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river origins to dense woodlands, and appreciate the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance routes that pass through Reinosa?
A: Yes, part of the extensive Natural Route of the Ebro (GR 99) passes through the Reinosa area. This long-distance path traces the Ebro river valley from its source to the Mediterranean. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e997027449/reinosa-fontibre-argueeso-por-el-natural-way-of-the-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reinosa - Fontibre - Argüeso por el Natural Way of the Ebro&lt;/a&gt; route covers a section of this significant trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Reinosa?
A: Reinosa offers appealing hiking experiences throughout much of the year. Autumn is particularly vibrant due to the dense beech forests in areas like Saja-Besaya Natural Park. Spring and summer provide pleasant temperatures for exploring, while winter hikes are also possible, though some higher altitude trails may require more experience and appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, Reinosa offers more demanding trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Bridge – Source of the Ebro River loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering 18.4 km with significant elevation changes. The region also features high peaks like Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares, which offer challenging routes and require good fitness.

Q: Can I combine hiking with historical exploration in Reinosa?
A: Absolutely. Reinosa's surroundings blend natural beauty with historical significance. The easy hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Roman city, is a great example. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; itself offers a glimpse into the area's past, which can be explored via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31885896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa – Plaza de España, Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Reinosa a good base for exploring other mountain ranges like Picos de Europa?
A: While not directly connected, Reinosa can serve as a potential base for exploring the wider Cantabrian region, including the renowned &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Picos_de_Europa_National_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picos de Europa National Park&lt;/a&gt;. The Picos are known for their dramatic mountains and exceptional footpaths, offering a different kind of alpine experience for those willing to travel a bit further.
tips:
On a hill, and guarding the road that linked the coast with Castilla through the Saja Valley, is the Castillo de San Vicente, in Argüeso, within the municipality of the Brotherhood of Campoo de Suso, in the South from Cantabria (Spain).
Built during the 13th, 14th and 15th centuries, it was the emblem and fortress of the dominion of the House of Mendoza in the highlands of Campoo.
The town of Fontibre is known because the source of the Ebro River, one of the most important in Spain, is located here.
Meeting place for neighbors and visitors.

The Teatro Principal is located at the end of Calle Mayor, next to the Fuente La Aurora. Although it was inaugurated in 1893; It underwent several subsequent reforms, the last being in 1985, which returned a large part of the original structure. Inside, it has a room with boxes and an amphitheater of seats on the upper deck. Outside, preserve its characteristic arches and the arcade they form.
Currently, it is owned by the Reinosa City Council, which organizes musical, theatrical and cinematographic activities in it.

(Text taken from aytodereinosa.es)
